description: Help topics consumed by both the web (window.hosHelp) and native (HelpRegistry) UIs. Each topic has a title and a body (array of paragraphs). HTML tags in body are allowed on web; native strips them. Adding a new topic here makes it available in both UIs next time they load.

real-sim.title: Real mode vs. Sim mode
body: HumanityOS has two contexts that swap which data your pages show.
Real is for your actual life. Task boards hold real projects, the marketplace shows real listings, inventory is what you actually own, maps show the real world around you.
Sim is for the simulation. Same pages, same tools, but the data comes from the game world. Practice farming, building, crafting, and trading before the stakes are real.
The same habits and skills apply to both. Flip the toggle any time. Your identity and wallet stay the same.

identity.title: Your identity
body: Your identity on HumanityOS is a cryptographic keypair generated on your device the first time you open the chat. No username, no password, no account form.
The key is self-custody: only you have it. Losing it means losing access, so back it up with the 24-word seed phrase in Settings.
The same key is also your wallet address, so your identity and your money are one and the same.
You can move between servers, reinstall the app, or switch devices: enter your seed phrase and you are yourself again.

wallet.title: The wallet
body: Your cryptographic key is also a Solana wallet address. You do not set one up separately.
From the Wallet page you can check your balance, send and receive payments, stake, see token swaps, and view transaction history.
Because the wallet is embedded in your identity, tipping or paying a user is as simple as knowing their name on the network.
You are responsible for your keys. Back up your seed phrase. Funds sent to a forgotten address are not recoverable.

federation.title: Federated servers
body: HumanityOS is federated, meaning there is no single central server. Anyone can run their own, and they talk to each other over a shared protocol.
If one server goes down, the rest of the network keeps working. If a server refuses to carry your voice, you can move to another or start your own.
Your identity works on every server in the network. Your profile replicates so people can recognise you even if they have never shared a server with you before.
To run your own: the desktop app ships with a headless mode that works as a full server. See the self-hosting guide in the docs.

humanity-accord.title: The Humanity Accord
body: The Humanity Accord is the philosophical foundation of the project: a short, plain-language statement of the values the platform is built around.
Core ideas: individual dignity, universal accountability, transparency, AI as participant rather than tool, and a commitment to ending poverty through capability rather than charity.
Every user of the platform, human or AI, is expected to act in the spirit of the Accord. It is not a legal document and has no enforcement arm other than community judgement.
Read the full text in the docs. It is worth fifteen minutes.

chat-context-menu.title: Right-click menu
body: Right-click on a message or a user to see what you can do.
User actions (red) are available to everyone: copy message text, reply, quote, view profile, start a DM.
Moderator actions (green) are available if you have mod role on the current channel: delete message, mute user, pin, timeout.
Admin actions (blue) are available if you are a server admin: ban, remove from server, view audit trail.
You only see the sections your role grants. Roles are per-server: you may be an admin on one and a regular user on another.

seed-phrase.title: Seed phrase backup
body: Your seed phrase is 24 words generated from your private key using the BIP39 standard.
Write it down on paper. Do not photograph it. Do not save it to cloud storage without encryption. Do not type it into a browser tab you did not open yourself.
Anyone with your seed phrase can act as you, spend your tokens, and read your DMs. Treat it with that much care.
If you lose access to this device, enter the seed phrase on a new install to recover your full identity, including message history backup if you had sync enabled.

onboarding.title: Getting started
body: New to HumanityOS? The Onboarding page walks through the four core concepts (identity, wallet, Real/Sim, federation), points to each major feature, and has quest chains you can tick off as you go.
You can reach it any time from the landing page, or by opening the /onboarding link directly.
Your progress is saved in this browser. Reset it from the Onboarding page if you want a fresh pass.
